title: [phase:repair] Patch the Codex task-completion backup scan to avoid expensive full-session audit work for non-task_complete events. Write only in these files if needed: /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py and /Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard...
summary_text: Implemented with minimal scope. I changed only: - [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py#L931) - [test_task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/tests/test_task_backup.py#L182) What changed: - In `scan_codex_task_completions`, I added a cheap pre-gate before expensive work: - Skip if event is not `payload.type == "task_complete"`. - Skip if `turn_id`/`timestamp` are invalid. - Compute `record_id` early and skip if it already exists. - Only after that gate, compute: - `_codex_audit_snapshot(...)` - `_recent_active_models(...)` - compliance downgrade for missing required external providers. Regression tests added: - `test_scan_non_task_complete_events_skip_expensive_audit` - Verifies non-`task_complete` events do not call `_codex_audit_snapshot` or `_recent_active_models`. - `test_scan_duplicate_task_complete_events_call_expensive_audit_once` - Verifies duplicate `task_complete` events in one scan record once and call expensive helpers only once. Validation: - `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_task_backup -v` - Result: all tests in `tests/test_task_backup.py` passed (26/26).
